text.title: Guardrails Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for the three-suite test harness.

rule.statement: The harness has three explicit suites: `tests/security/` (must-block), `tests/accuracy/` (must-pass), `tests/perf/` (latency + throughput). Each suite has independent fail criteria.
rule.rationale: Conflating security and accuracy hides the real signal — a high-FP run still passes "no leak" but breaks UX. Separate suites force separate reporting.

rule.statement: Every detector (PII, injection, etc.) is unit-tested via `@pytest.mark.parametrize` with at least 10 cases covering positive, negative, edge, and Unicode inputs.
rule.rationale: Hand-rolled per-detector tests miss the input-space variance; parametrize forces a table you can read at a glance and regression-protect.

rule.statement: CI tests mock every LLM client. No live API calls in any pytest run. Live red-team runs go through a `--live` marker that CI skips by default.
rule.rationale: Live LLM in CI multiplies the build cost by 100x and adds non-determinism that destroys flake tracking.

rule.statement: Adversarial payload library lives at `tests/payloads/v.jsonl`. Each payload has `id`, `category`, `attack_string`, `expected_outcome`. New payloads bump version; old payloads stay for regression.
rule.rationale: Mutating a single file destroys the regression baseline; versioned files preserve "this attack used to bypass; we fixed it" memory.

rule.statement: Accuracy suite declares a false-positive budget (default ≤1% of legit inputs blocked). Exceeding the budget fails the suite even if every security test passes.
rule.rationale: A 5% FP rate destroys users' trust faster than any single bypass. The budget makes FP a first-class fail criterion.

rule.statement: Perf suite stores a baseline `bench.json` per release. Current run fails if p99 latency exceeds baseline by > 20% or if throughput drops by > 15%.
rule.rationale: Without a baseline, slow drift never trips an alarm and one day the SLO is gone. Pinned baselines catch regression at the commit that introduced it.
